module.name: ast/navigation
module.description: Higher-level AST navigation helpers for ancestry and sibling traversal.

scenarios.name: should return the nearest ancestor that matches the predicate
scenarios.given: a node whose parent chain includes an ancestor accepted by the predicate
scenarios.then: returns the nearest matching ancestor node

scenarios.name: should return null when no ancestor matches the predicate
scenarios.given: a node whose parent chain does not include a matching ancestor

scenarios.name: should return the nearest enclosing function-like ancestor
scenarios.given: a node nested inside an arrow function, function declaration, or function expression
scenarios.then: returns the nearest enclosing function-like node

scenarios.name: should return null when no function-like ancestor exists
scenarios.given: a node outside of any function-like ancestor

scenarios.name: should return the following statement in a block
scenarios.given: a block statement containing a statement followed by another statement
scenarios.then: returns the next statement in the block body

scenarios.name: should return null when the statement is the last block entry
scenarios.given: a block statement whose target statement has no following sibling

scenarios.name: should return the parent block statement for a statement node
scenarios.given: a statement whose parent is a BlockStatement

scenarios.name: should return null when the parent is not a block statement
scenarios.given: a statement whose parent is not a BlockStatement

scenarios.name: should return true when a matching boundary is reached before a stop boundary
scenarios.given: an ancestor chain where the nearest relevant boundary type is in the match set

scenarios.name: should return false when a stop boundary is reached before a matching boundary
scenarios.given: an ancestor chain where the nearest relevant boundary type is in the stop set
